reviews.review_title: Id give negative 5 stars if I could
reviews.review_description: I bought the game hoping for a vr racing game, I mean it’s $30 and you can’t even control the car with your hands only joystick. Graphics are also terrible and the worst part? I didn’t even finish the first race before I decided to close it an request a refund. Surprise surprise, refunds aren’t available for this game. DO NOT BUY!!! There’s better racing games and they’re 1/3 the price or free.

As a person who loved all the Grid Games for many years… this one is controlling.. it doesn’t let you play to win, it forces you to lose unless you pay for the upgrades… which they used to do on console gaming and I never paid, destroyed and got banned from the old versions because I went online and beat the game, online users and never spent a single penny!! Unfortunately this game follows the same agenda!!  If the devs want to try me, I am willing to bring this to real life and show them in on of my many cars I have owned to show them what real racing is!!

I was a bit hesitant on purchasing this game because of the low reviews. I was pleasantly surprised at how fun this game was. The graphics aren’t t rhe best compared to PC. This port didn’t take full advantage of Meta 3’s capabilities but it’s a nice little game and one of the better racers on Meta.

*Update:
Game was updated and sliders were added to the options, graphics selection. Now that you can choose the resolution and refresh rate in game, the requested bump in graphics makes this a must have game. Its is not better looking than a PS5 game but it is now the most fun racer I've owned on any system, and the multiplayer is easy and fun!

**
I recently started playing the Xbox One version, so my expectations were high. Unfortunately, the game looks like Gran Turismo 3 in VR. The first race is garish and ugly but the game gets better looking on different tracks with different cars, and various weather effects.

The driving is great, the most fun I've had in VR racing and that includes PCVR titles. The racing is smooth, although it's arcade action,  not simulation. 

If the graphics could be improved a few steps,  it would be a top notch game.

reviews.review_title: Best cae game on Quest by far
reviews.review_description: Dont listen to the bad critics, this game is BY FAR the best car game. I regret not buying it sooner! A lot of different cars, official brands, good engine sounds, good speed sensation, a lot of détails in the background and the scenes, good storyline for solo mode, car interiors, you can even get an enemy when you crash the other AI cars... This is a real AAA racing game, just update the graphics résolution in the settings as soon as you can.

The game is fun and the racing is tight. The resolution and refresh rate sliders is nice as well.

The only issues I have is that you have to use the joysticks for turning and the lack of steering wheel support. Also some of the game modes seem to crash the whole game at times. Trying to get into an online race never seems to work for me. I’ll definitely be double dipping this title when the Switch 2 version comes out so I can use my steering wheel for it.

Would love to see some other Codemasters games come out like some rally games!

reviews.review_title: Looks great With Resolution turned up.
reviews.review_description:
This is the best racing game I have found for standalone, the tracks are interesting and haven't gotten boring yet.
  The game features a nemesis system (even offline) so AI racers might actually try to wreck you if you push them too much.
  There are many different cars to race in, even if you don't own a car, you can loan it for half race rewards. Meaning you can race with players who have car classes you don't own.
  Every car definitely FEELS different. At one point in the story, there's a car that almost flipped on turns.
  I wish there was a mode for hand control on steering, since the joystick is a little to small to make smooth low curve turns. Other than that, the game is great, completely worth the money, and I haven't even played online yet.

Hi 

The steering is horrible. Who want steering by joystick or one hand with controller?
Even childs can make steering by two controller like you have real wheel in the hand in VR chat.
Please make normal steering I mean you holding two controller with hand and normal rotate like in real life. This game could be so good and you destroy all of this by horrible and stupid steering model :(

After you beat the first race, you can go to the settings on quest3 and turn up the graphics quality to high!
The game looks very good this way on a standalone!
It's by far the best racer on standalone headsets!
You even have a campaign mode with a story and so many race cars and classes!

Name one other fully featured racing game on the Quest platform, I’ll wait……

Can’t think of one? Well, that is because there is no other racing game with this amount of single player content that is on the Quest.

The graphics are not the best, that is where most of the complaints come from usually from Q2 users. 

If you use QGO “Quest Games Optimizer”, tweak the resolution, the horsepower of your Quest, also add the reflections, and bump up the resolution in the in game settings you can get this to look a lot better, and totally acceptable for a portable VR platform. 

This game is not even close to being as bad as all the reviews said it was. I think it’s pretty perfect after doing a little tweaking, especially on a mobile platform. You get the sense of speed, exhilaration, and all of the content from a triple AAA racing game on the go. 

Remember, this game has so much content they needed to make cuts in certain areas so some of the textures are really blurry in the background but when you’re in a race, you don’t even really notice it at all. You’re too busy trying to stay in first place and when you are locked in, it really sells itself well.

If you own a Quest 3/3S and tweak your games using QGO to get the most out of them, then I highly recommend it.

reviews.review_title: Fantastic game! No clue why low ratings
reviews.review_description: Perhaps this game wasnt great at release, and thats why it seems to have a low score? I didnt buy or try this game until August 2023, but I absolutely love it! Others have noted that this game is great and worth a shot as well. You can always return the game within a short period of time if you dont like it... Im not sure if people just have really high expectations and think that this game should look like its running on a high-end gaming PC like most AAA titles? The game does seem to have a little more pixelation than other VR games, but at the same time this game has a lot of amazing detail and shape and tons of things going on all at once (whereas many other VR games on Quest 2 that look more polished also have way more cartoony-looks and have a lot less detail, so theres give and take here). In fact, Im super impressed at how well this game performs and looks on the Quest 2, considering the hardware is now several years old cell-phone type computing. Its not easy to get this many cars on the track racing all at once, I recall that even Gran Turismo on PSP only had like 4 opponents to race with due to lack of performance on the hardware...and this Grid VR game has tons of opponents on the track that youre racing against! I bet that this game will look even better on the Quest 3. For me, the immersion in this game is great, the controls are tight, the gameplay is fun, I enjoy the graphics and scenery, and Im super happy to own this game. I love looking around while Im driving, and the physics feel real/decent enough to me that Im actually in the car and driving it. A+++ and I think that its worth a shot, especially if you enjoy racing, the Grid series, or any of its competitor racing series that are out there. There isnt anything better than this for the Quest 2 in terms of a first-person racing sim, unless you want to plug your Quest 2 into your computer and play PC racing in VR. I hope this game's ratings continue to improve, because it deserves at least a solid 4 stars or more. Only suggestion I have that I think would be cool, is to be able to use your hands to steer the wheel, but at the same time that may becime exhausting after a few races...perhals the devs can make a setting to toggle between wheel steering and thumbstick for steering.

reviews.review_title: Excellent racing game, better in VR
reviews.review_description: GRID Legends is the only real racing game on the Quest platform, which is a bummer because racing is one of my top genres. Thankfully the only racing game on the platform is a fantastic one. GRID Legends is a full console quality (in terms of gameplay and content, not in terms of graphics) game that runs untethered on the Quest. If you love racing there is hundreds of hours of content here with tons of modes, tracks, cars, and various kinds of progression and unlocks. I don’t play online multiplayer in games so I can’t speak to that, but there’s a full $60 console or PC game worth of racing here. And in VR it is far more immersive than flat games. The game only supports the Quest controllers. That bothers some people but not me. The whole reason I have a Quest is so I don’t have to mess with wires and tons of equipment and peripherals, so I’m good with the controls. They are comfortable, responsive, and work great. The only thing that isn’t nearly perfect about this game are the graphics. This is a console / PC game scaled down to Quest. That means it isn’t built with the Quest platform in mind and is therefore not graphically well optimized for the platform. The result are graphics that look like a PS2 game,  maybe even a bit worse than a PS2 game. It looks like Daytona USA in my opinion. The Quest 3 is capable of a lot more than what this game does graphically, but I don’t think their engine can scale down to the Quest and retain graphical features. Hopefully someday a developer will build a full racing simcade game for the platform. I believe it would be possible to have this level of gameplay with significantly better graphics if the game was developed for the platform from the ground up. Still, even with the poor visuals the game is incredible. Just pretend it’s some trendy Indy game going for stylized retro visuals. Overall this is an excellent game and a literal must purchase if you like racing games.

The Game looks amazing on the quest 3 i love it But…. i wish it has gamepad support that i can connect a controller or racing wheel to play it this way it would be more imersive please bring an update for racing wheel and gamepad support

For two years now, I’ve seen panic and drama surrounding this game — and for two years I’ve continued to enjoy playing it. So here’s some plain info for those still hesitating:

1. The game is completely stable. Crashes only happen when trying to enter online mode (yes, unfortunately online multiplayer doesn’t work). Otherwise, no issues.

2. There are some graphical glitches, and the default resolution is low. However, Quest 3 can handle much higher resolution, so just increase it in the settings.

3. Yes, you can’t grab the steering wheel with your hands here — you have to use the stick. But the thing is, even if hand steering were available, you’d end up switching to the stick anyway. Holding your hands in the air during long races is just unbearable (as anyone who's played other racing games can confirm).

4. This is a full port of the desktop version of Grid Legends, including the story mode, tons of tracks and cars, intense gameplay, and great driving physics.

Bottom line: if you're a fan of true racing sims — go for it, you won’t regret it. Despite some visual glitches and the broken online mode, this game is fantastic.
